verses.latin_vulgate: Post aliquantulum autem temporis, cum dies triticeæ messis instarent, venit Samson, invisere volens uxorem suam, et attulit ei hædum de capris. Cumque cubiculum ejus solito vellet intrare, prohibuit eum pater illius, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֨י מִיָּמִ֜ים בִּימֵ֣י קְצִיר־חִטִּ֗ים וַיִּפְקֹ֨ד שִׁמְשׁ֤וֹן אֶת־אִשְׁתּוֹ֙ בִּגְדִ֣י עִזִּ֔ים וַיֹּ֕אמֶר אָבֹ֥אָה אֶל־אִשְׁתִּ֖י הֶחָ֑דְרָה וְלֹֽא־נְתָנ֥וֹ אָבִ֖יהָ לָבֽוֹא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And a while after, when the days of the wheat harvest were at hand, Samson came, meaning to visit his wife, and he brought her a kid of the flock. And when he would have gone into her chamber, as usual, her father would not suffer him, saying:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tapahtui muutamain päiväin perästä, kuin nisun elonaika lähestyi, tuli Simson oppimaan emäntäänsä ja toi hänelle vohlan, ja hän sanoi: minä menen emäntäni tykö makaushuoneesen; ja hänen emäntänsä isä ei sallinut hänen sinne mennä,
verses.king_james: But it came to pass within a while after, in the time of wheat harvest, that Samson visited his wife with a kid; and he said, I will go in to my wife into the chamber. But her father would not suffer him to go in.

verses.latin_vulgate: Putavi quod odisses eam, et ideo tradidi illam amico tuo : sed habet sororem, quæ junior et pulchrior illa est : sit tibi pro ea uxor.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אָבִ֗יהָ אָמֹ֤ר אָמַ֙רְתִּי֙ כִּי־שָׂנֹ֣א שְׂנֵאתָ֔הּ וָאֶתְּנֶ֖נָּה לְמֵרֵעֶ֑ךָ הֲלֹ֨א אֲחוֹתָ֤הּ הַקְּטַנָּה֙ טוֹבָ֣ה מִמֶּ֔נָּה תְּהִי־נָ֥א לְךָ֖ תַּחְתֶּֽיהָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: I thought thou hadst hated her, and therefore I gave her to thy friend: but she hath a sister, who is younger and fairer than she, take her to wife instead of her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta sanoi: minä luulin todella, sinun kokonansa vihastuneen hänen päällensä, ja annoin hänen sinun ystävälles. Mutta hänellä on toinen nuorempi sisar, kauniimpi muotoansa kuin hän, olkoon se nyt sinulla tämän edestä.
verses.king_james: And her father said, I verily thought that thou hadst utterly hated her; therefore I gave her to thy companion: is not her younger sister fairer than she? take her, I pray thee, instead of her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui Samson respondit : Ab hac die non erit culpa in me contra Philisthæos : faciam enim vobis mala.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לָהֶם֙ שִׁמְשׁ֔וֹן נִקֵּ֥יתִי הַפַּ֖עַם מִפְּלִשְׁתִּ֑ים כִּי־עֹשֶׂ֥ה אֲנִ֛י עִמָּ֖ם רָעָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Samson answered him: From this day I shall be blameless in what I do against the Philistines: for I will do you evils.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Simson sanoi heille: nyt on minulla oikia syy Philistealaisia vastaan: minä teen heille vahinkoa.
verses.king_james: And Samson said concerning them, Now shall I be more blameless than the Philistines, though I do them a displeasure.

verses.latin_vulgate: Perrexitque et cepit trecentas vulpes, caudasque earum junxit ad caudas, et faces ligavit in medio :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֣לֶךְ שִׁמְשׁ֔וֹן וַיִּלְכֹּ֖ד שְׁלֹשׁ־מֵא֣וֹת שׁוּעָלִ֑ים וַיִּקַּ֣ח לַפִּדִ֗ים וַיֶּ֤פֶן זָנָב֙ אֶל־זָנָ֔ב וַיָּ֨שֶׂם לַפִּ֥יד אֶחָ֛ד בֵּין־שְׁנֵ֥י הַזְּנָב֖וֹת בַּתָּֽוֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he went and caught three hundred foxes, and coupled them tail to tail, and fastened torches between the tails:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Simson meni ja otti kolmesataa kettua kiinni, ja otti kekäleet, ja käänsi hännät yhteen, ja sitoi aina yhden kekäleen keskelle kahden hännän välille,
verses.king_james: And Samson went and caught three hundred foxes, and took firebrands, and turned tail to tail, and put a firebrand in the midst between two tails.

verses.latin_vulgate: quas igne succendens, dimisit ut huc illucque discurrerent. Quæ statim perrexerunt in segetes Philisthinorum. Quibus succensis, et comportatæ jam fruges, et adhuc stantes in stipula, concrematæ sunt, in tantum ut vineas quoque et oliveta flamma consumeret.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּבְעֶר־אֵשׁ֙ בַּלַּפִּידִ֔ים וַיְשַׁלַּ֖ח בְּקָמ֣וֹת פְּלִשְׁתִּ֑ים וַיַּבְעֵ֛ר מִגָּדִ֥ישׁ וְעַד־קָמָ֖ה וְעַד־כֶּ֥רֶם זָֽיִת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And setting them on fire he let the foxes go, that they might run about hither and thither. And they presently went into the standing corn of the Philistines. Which being set on fire, both the corn that was already carried together, and that which was yet standing, was all burnt, insomuch that the flame consumed also the vineyards and the oliveyards.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sytytti tulen kekäleihin, ja päästi ne Philistealaisten eloon; ja ne polttivat heidän kykäänsä ja kuhilaansa, niin myös viinapuut ja öljypuut.
verses.king_james: And when he had set the brands on fire, he let them go into the standing corn of the Philistines, and burnt up both the shocks, and also the standing corn, with the vineyards and olives.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixeruntque Philisthiim : Quis fecit hanc rem ? Quibus dictum est : Samson gener Thamnathæi : quia tulit uxorem ejus, et alteri tradidit, hæc operatus est. Ascenderuntque Philisthiim, et combusserunt tam mulierem quam patrem ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ פְלִשְׁתִּים֮ מִ֣י עָ֣שָׂה זֹאת֒ וַיֹּאמְר֗וּ שִׁמְשׁוֹן֙ חֲתַ֣ן הַתִּמְנִ֔י כִּ֚י לָקַ֣ח אֶת־אִשְׁתּ֔וֹ וַֽיִּתְּנָ֖הּ לְמֵרֵעֵ֑הוּ וַיַּעֲל֣וּ פְלִשְׁתִּ֔ים וַיִּשְׂרְפ֥וּ אוֹתָ֛הּ וְאֶת־אָבִ֖יהָ בָּאֵֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then the Philistines said: Who hath done this thing? And it was answered: Samson, the son in law of the Thamnathite, because he took away his wife, and gave her to another, hath done these things. And the Philistines went up and burnt both the woman and her father.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoivat Philistealaiset: kuka on tämän tehnyt? Niin sanottiin: Simson, Timnalaisen vävy, että hän otti häneltä emännän pois ja antoi sen hänen ystävällensä. Niin Philistealaiset menivät ylös ja polttivat sekä vaimon että hänen isänsä.
verses.king_james: Then the Philistines said, Who hath done this? And they answered, Samson, the son in law of the Timnite, because he had taken his wife, and given her to his companion. And the Philistines came up, and burnt her and her father with fire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quibus ait Samson : Licet hæc feceritis, tamen adhuc ex vobis expetam ultionem, et tunc quiescam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לָהֶם֙ שִׁמְשׁ֔וֹן אִֽם־תַּעֲשׂ֖וּן כָּזֹ֑את כִּ֛י אִם־נִקַּ֥מְתִּי בָכֶ֖ם וְאַחַ֥ר אֶחְדָּֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Samson said to them: Although you have done this, yet will I be revenged of you, and then I will be quiet.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Simson sanoi heille: ja vaikka te tämän teitte, kuitenkin minä teille kostan ja sitte lakkaan.
verses.king_james: And Samson said unto them, Though ye have done this, yet will I be avenged of you, and after that I will cease.

verses.latin_vulgate: Percussitque eos ingenti plaga, ita ut stupentes suram femori imponerent. Et descendens habitavit in spelunca petræ Etam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֨ךְ אוֹתָ֥ם שׁ֛וֹק עַל־יָרֵ֖ךְ מַכָּ֣ה גְדוֹלָ֑ה וַיֵּ֣רֶד וַיֵּ֔שֶׁב בִּסְעִ֖יף סֶ֥לַע עֵיטָֽם׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And he made a great slaughter of them, so that in astonishment they laid the calf of the leg upon the thigh. And going down he dwelt in a cavern of the rock Etam.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän löi heitä sangen kovasti sekä hartioihin että kupeisiin, ja meni sieltä alas asumaan Etamin mäen rotkoon.
verses.king_james: And he smote them hip and thigh with a great slaughter: and he went down and dwelt in the top of the rock Etam.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur ascendentes Philisthiim in terram Juda, castrametati sunt in loco, qui postea vocatus est Lechi, id est, Maxilla, ubi eorum effusus est exercitus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּעֲל֣וּ פְלִשְׁתִּ֔ים וַֽיַּחֲנ֖וּ בִּֽיהוּדָ֑ה וַיִּנָּטְשׁ֖וּ בַּלֶּֽחִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then the Philistines going up into the land of Juda, camped in the place which afterwards was called Lechi, that is, the Jawbone, where their army was spread abroad.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Philistealaiset menivät ylös ja sioittivat itsensä Juudan maalle ja levittivät heitänsä Lehiin.
verses.king_james: Then the Philistines went up, and pitched in Judah, and spread themselves in Lehi.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixeruntque ad eos de tribu Juda : Cur ascendistis adversum nos ? Qui responderunt : Ut ligemus Samson venimus, et reddamus ei quæ in nos operatus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּֽאמְרוּ֙ אִ֣ישׁ יְהוּדָ֔ה לָמָ֖ה עֲלִיתֶ֣ם עָלֵ֑ינוּ וַיֹּאמְר֗וּ לֶאֱס֤וֹר אֶת־שִׁמְשׁוֹן֙ עָלִ֔ינוּ לַעֲשׂ֣וֹת ל֔וֹ כַּאֲשֶׁ֖ר עָ֥שָׂה לָֽנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the men of the tribe of Juda said to them: Why are you come up against us? They answered: We are come to bind Samson, and to pay him for what he hath done against us.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Juudan miehet sanoivat: miksi olette tänne tulleet meitä vastaan? He vastasivat: me olemme tänne tulleet sitomaan Simsonia ja tekemään hänelle, niinkuin hän meille teki.
verses.king_james: And the men of Judah said, Why are ye come up against us? And they answered, To bind Samson are we come up, to do to him as he hath done to us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Descenderunt ergo tria millia virorum de Juda ad specum silicis Etam, dixeruntque ad Samson : Nescis quod Philisthiim imperent nobis ? quare hoc facere voluisti ? Quibus ille ait : Sicut fecerunt mihi, sic feci eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּרְד֡וּ שְׁלֹ֩שֶׁת֩ אֲלָפִ֨ים אִ֜ישׁ מִֽיהוּדָ֗ה אֶל־סְעִיף֮ סֶ֣לַע עֵיטָם֒ וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ לְשִׁמְשׁ֗וֹן הֲלֹ֤א יָדַ֙עְתָּ֙ כִּֽי־מֹשְׁלִ֥ים בָּ֙נוּ֙ פְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים וּמַה־זֹּ֖את עָשִׂ֣יתָ לָּ֑נוּ וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לָהֶ֔ם כַּֽאֲשֶׁר֙ עָ֣שׂוּ לִ֔י כֵּ֖ן עָשִׂ֥יתִי לָהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Wherefore three thousand men of Juda went down to the cave of the rock Etam, and said to Samson: Knowest thou not that the Philistines rule over us? Why wouldst thou do thus? And he said to them: As they did to me, so have I done to them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin meni kolmetuhatta miestä Juudasta Etamin mäen rotkoon, ja sanoivat Simsonille: etkös tiedä Philistealaisten hallitsevan meitä? kuinka sinä olet tämän tehnyt meitä vastaan? Ja hän sanoi heille: niinkuin he tekivät minulle, niin minä tein heille.
verses.king_james: Then three thousand men of Judah went to the top of the rock Etam, and said to Samson, Knowest thou not that the Philistines are rulers over us? what is this that thou hast done unto us? And he said unto them, As they did unto me, so have I done unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ligare, inquiunt, te venimus, et tradere in manus Philisthinorum. Quibus Samson : Jurate, ait, et spondete mihi quod non occidatis me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמְרוּ לוֹ֙ לֶאֱסׇרְךָ֣ יָרַ֔דְנוּ לְתִתְּךָ֖ בְּיַד־פְּלִשְׁתִּ֑ים וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לָהֶם֙ שִׁמְשׁ֔וֹן הִשָּׁבְע֣וּ לִ֔י פֶּֽן־תִּפְגְּע֥וּן בִּ֖י אַתֶּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they said to him: We are come to bind thee, and to deliver thee into the hands of the Philistines. And Samson said to them: Swear to me, and promise me that you will not kill me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he sanoivat hänelle: me olemme tulleet sinua sitomaan ja antamaan Philistealaisten käsiin. Simson sanoi heille: vannokaat siis minulle, ettette karkaa minun päälleni.
verses.king_james: And they said unto him, We are come down to bind thee, that we may deliver thee into the hand of the Philistines. And Samson said unto them, Swear unto me, that ye will not fall upon me yourselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerunt : Non te occidemus, sed vinctum trademus. Ligaveruntque eum duobus novis funibus, et tulerunt eum de petra Etam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֧אמְרוּ ל֣וֹ לֵאמֹ֗ר לֹ֚א כִּֽי־אָסֹ֤ר נֶאֱסׇרְךָ֙ וּנְתַנּ֣וּךָ בְיָדָ֔ם וְהָמֵ֖ת לֹ֣א נְמִיתֶ֑ךָ וַיַּאַסְרֻ֗הוּ בִּשְׁנַ֙יִם֙ עֲבֹתִ֣ים חֲדָשִׁ֔ים וַֽיַּעֲל֖וּהוּ מִן־הַסָּֽלַע׃
verses.douay_rheims: They said: We will not kill thee: but we will deliver thee up bound. And they bound him with two new cords, and brought him from the rock Etam.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he sanoivat hänelle: emme karkaa sinun päälles, mutta ainoasti sidomme sinun tukevasti ja annamme sinun heidän käsiinsä, ja emme sinua kuoliaaksi lyö. Ja he sitoivat hänen kahdella uudella nuoralla ja veivät hänen kukkulalta pois.
verses.king_james: And they spake unto him, saying, No; but we will bind thee fast, and deliver thee into their hand: but surely we will not kill thee. And they bound him with two new cords, and brought him up from the rock.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui cum venisset ad locum Maxillæ, et Philisthiim vociferantes occurrissent ei, irruit spiritus Domini in eum : et sicut solent ad odorem ignis lina consumi, ita vincula, quibus ligatus erat, dissipata sunt et soluta.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הוּא־בָ֣א עַד־לֶ֔חִי וּפְלִשְׁתִּ֖ים הֵרִ֣יעוּ לִקְרָאת֑וֹ וַתִּצְלַ֨ח עָלָ֜יו ר֣וּחַ יְהֹוָ֗ה וַתִּהְיֶ֨ינָה הָעֲבֹתִ֜ים אֲשֶׁ֣ר עַל־זְרוֹעוֹתָ֗יו כַּפִּשְׁתִּים֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בָּעֲר֣וּ בָאֵ֔שׁ וַיִּמַּ֥סּוּ אֱסוּרָ֖יו מֵעַ֥ל יָדָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now when he was come to the place of the Jawbone, and the Philistines shouting went to meet him, the Spirit of the Lord came strongly upon him: and as flax is wont to be consumed at the approach of fire, so the bands with which he was bound were broken and loosed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin hän tuli Lehiin, ilakoitsivat Philistealaiset häntä vastaan; mutta Herran henki tuli voimalliseksi hänessä, ja nuorat hänen käsivarsissansa tulivat niinkuin poltettu lanka, ja niin raukesivat siteet hänen käsistänsä.
verses.king_james: And when he came unto Lehi, the Philistines shouted against him: and the Spirit of the Lord came mightily upon him, and the cords that were upon his arms became as flax that was burnt with fire, and his bands loosed from off his hands.

verses.latin_vulgate: Inventamque maxillam, id est, mandibulam asini, quæ jacebat, arripiens interfecit in ea mille viros,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּמְצָ֥א לְחִי־חֲמ֖וֹר טְרִיָּ֑ה וַיִּשְׁלַ֤ח יָדוֹ֙ וַיִּקָּחֶ֔הָ וַיַּךְ־בָּ֖הּ אֶ֥לֶף אִֽישׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And finding a jawbone, even the jawbone of an ass, which lay there, catching it up, he slew therewith a thousand men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän löysi tuoreen aasin leukaluun, ja hän ojensi kätensä, otti sen ja löi sillä tuhannen miestä.
verses.king_james: And he found a new jawbone of an ass, and put forth his hand, and took it, and slew a thousand men therewith.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ait :   In maxilla asini,  in mandibula pulli asinarum,  delevi eos,  et percussi mille viros.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר שִׁמְשׁ֔וֹן בִּלְחִ֣י הַחֲמ֔וֹר חֲמ֖וֹר חֲמֹרָתָ֑יִם בִּלְחִ֣י הַחֲמ֔וֹר הִכֵּ֖יתִי אֶ֥לֶף אִֽישׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said: With the jawbone of an ass, with the jaw of the colt of asses, I have destroyed them, and have slain a thousand men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Simson sanoi: katso, siinä he koossa makaavat, yhdellä leukaluulla löin minä tuhannen miestä.
verses.king_james: And Samson said, With the jawbone of an ass, heaps upon heaps, with the jaw of an ass have I slain a thousand men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque hæc verba canens complesset, projecit mandibulam de manu, et vocavit nomen loci illius Ramathlechi, quod interpretatur, Elevatio maxillæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְהִי֙ כְּכַלֹּת֣וֹ לְדַבֵּ֔ר וַיַּשְׁלֵ֥ךְ הַלְּחִ֖י מִיָּד֑וֹ וַיִּקְרָ֛א לַמָּק֥וֹם הַה֖וּא רָ֥מַת לֶֽחִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when he had ended these words, singing, he threw the jawbone out of his hand, and called the name of that place Ramathlechi, which is interpreted the lifting up of the jawbone.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin hän sen sanonut oli, heitti hän leukaluun kädestänsä pois, ja kutsui sen paikan RamatLehi.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when he had made an end of speaking, that he cast away the jawbone out of his hand, and called that place Ramath–lehi.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sitiensque valde, clamavit ad Dominum, et ait : Tu dedisti in manu servi tui salutem hanc maximam atque victoriam : en siti morior, incidamque in manus incircumcisorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּצְמָא֮ מְאֹד֒ וַיִּקְרָ֤א אֶל־יְהֹוָה֙ וַיֹּאמַ֔ר אַתָּה֙ נָתַ֣תָּ בְיַֽד־עַבְדְּךָ֔ אֶת־הַתְּשׁוּעָ֥ה הַגְּדֹלָ֖ה הַזֹּ֑את וְעַתָּה֙ אָמ֣וּת בַּצָּמָ֔א וְנָפַלְתִּ֖י בְּיַ֥ד הָעֲרֵלִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And being very thirsty, he cried to the Lord, and said: Thou hast given this very great deliverance and victory into the hand of thy servant: and behold I die for thirst, and shall fall into the hands of the uncircumcised.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin hän kovin janosi, huusi hän Herran tykö ja sanoi: sinä annoit tämän suuren pelastuksen palvelias käden kautta, ja nyt minä janoon kuolen ja lankeen ympärileikkaamattomain käsiin.
verses.king_james: And he was sore athirst, and called on the Lord, and said, Thou hast given this great deliverance into the hand of thy servant: and now shall I die for thirst, and fall into the hand of the uncircumcised?

verses.latin_vulgate: Aperuit itaque Dominus molarem dentem in maxilla asini, et egressæ sunt ex eo aquæ. Quibus haustis, refocillavit spiritum, et vires recepit. Idcirco appellatum est nomen loci illius, Fons invocantis de maxilla, usque in præsentem diem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּבְקַ֨ע אֱלֹהִ֜ים אֶת־הַמַּכְתֵּ֣שׁ אֲשֶׁר־בַּלֶּ֗חִי וַיֵּצְא֨וּ מִמֶּ֤נּוּ מַ֙יִם֙ וַיֵּ֔שְׁתְּ וַתָּ֥שׇׁב רוּח֖וֹ וַיֶּ֑חִי עַל־כֵּ֣ן ׀ קָרָ֣א שְׁמָ֗הּ עֵ֤ין הַקּוֹרֵא֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בַּלֶּ֔חִי עַ֖ד הַיּ֥וֹם הַזֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then the Lord opened a great tooth in the jaw of the ass and waters issued out of it. And when he had drunk them, he refreshed his spirit, and recovered his strength. Therefore the name of that place was called The Spring of him that invoked from the jawbone, until this present day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Jumala avasi syömähampaan leukaluussa, ja siitä vuosi vesi, ja kuin hän oli juonut, sai hän henkensä takaisin ja virkosi; sentähden kutsutaan myös se paikka vielä tähän päivään asti Huutajan lähteeksi, joka oli leukaluussa.
verses.king_james: But God clave an hollow place that was in the jaw, and there came water thereout; and when he had drunk, his spirit came again, and he revived: wherefore he called the name thereof En–hakkore, which is in Lehi unto this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Judicavitque Israël in diebus Philisthiim viginti annis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁפֹּ֧ט אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֛ל בִּימֵ֥י פְלִשְׁתִּ֖ים עֶשְׂרִ֥ים שָׁנָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And he judged Israel, in the days of the Philistines, twenty years.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän tuomitsi Israelia Philistealaisten aikana kaksikymmentä ajastaikaa.
verses.king_james: And he judged Israel in the days of the Philistines twenty years.
